App.name: App
App.desc: This class contains specific application details, such as its version and installation folders. It can be accessed with the app object, found in the automatically imported td module.

Attribute.name: Attribute
Attribute.desc:
An Attribute describes a general geometric Attribute, associated with a Prim Class, Point Class, or Vertex Class.
Specific values for each Prim, Point or Vertex are described with the AttributeData Class.
Lists of attributes for the SOP are described with the Attributes Class.

Attributes.name: Attributes
Attributes.desc: An Attributes object describes a set of Prim Class, Point Class, or Vertex Class attributes, contained within a SOP.

CHOP.name: CHOP
CHOP.desc: A CHOP describes a reference to a CHOP operator, containing a set of channels accessed with the Channel Class.

met.r: if True, allow  Utility nodes  to be returned. If False, Utility nodes will be ignored.       b   =   op  (  'project1'  )

COMP.name: COMP
COMP.desc: A COMP describes a reference to a Component operator.

Cell.name: Cell
Cell.desc:
The Cell Class describes the contents of a single cell from a DAT operator table.
The DAT Class offers many ways of accessing its individual cells.
DAT cells are always internally stored as strings, but may be accessed as numeric values.

met.s: run(*args, endFrame=False, fromOP=None, asParameter=False, group=None, delayFrames=0, delayMilliSeconds=0, delayRef=None)

Channel.name: Channel
Channel.desc:
A Channel object describes a single channel from a CHOP.  The CHOP Class provides many ways of accessing its individual channels.
See Working with CHOPs in Python for more examples of how to use this class.

Connector.name: Connector
Connector.desc:
The Connector class describes the input or output connection point of an operator.  There are two types of connections:  those between Components, and those between regular operators.
Connections between regular operators can be accessed through the OP.inputConnectors and OP.outputConnectors members...

DAT.name: DAT
DAT.desc:
A DAT describes a reference to a DAT operator.
This class inherits from the  OP class.

met.s: run(arg1, arg2..., endFrame=False, fromOP=None, asParameter=False, group=None, delayFrames=0, delayMilliSeconds=0, delayRef=me)

Dependency.name: Dependency
Dependency.desc: Dependency is the Procedural mechanism in TouchDesigner, where if one piece of data changes, it automatically causes other operators and expressions to re-Cook. This assures all data is consistent in a TouchDesigner process, and causes all the output displays, UIs, devices and protocols to update in...

Error_DAT.name: Error DAT
Error_DAT.desc: The Error DAT lists the most recent TouchDesigner errors in its FIFO (first in/first out) table.

Group.name: Group
Group.desc: An Group describes groups lists of Prim Class or Point Class.

License.name: License
License.desc: The License class describes a single instance of an installed license.  They can be accessed from the licenses object.

MAT.name: MAT
MAT.desc: A MAT describes a reference to a MAT operator.

MOD.name: MOD
MOD.desc: The MOD class provides access to TouchDesigner module functionality, allowing you to work with Python modules and extensions within TouchDesigner.

Matrix.name: Matrix
Matrix.desc:
The matrix class holds a single 4x4 matrix for use in transformations. The matrix's data layout is in column-major format, which is to say that the matrix is multiplied from the left of vectors and positions. The translation values are stored in the last column of the matrix.
A matrix is created wit...

met.s: mapUnitSquareToQuad(blX, blY, brX, brY, tlX, tlY, trX, trY)
mapQuadToUnitSquare(blX, blY, brX, brY, tlX, tlY, trX, trY)
projectionFrustum(left, right, bottom, top, near, far)
projectionStereo(ipd, convergeZ, fovX, aspectX, aspectY, near, far, rightEye = false)

met.r: > (1,1) space to a space defined by an arbitrary quadrilateral (blX, blY) -> (trX, trY). The 4 corners of the quadrilateral are given ('bl' means bottom left, 'tr' means top right etc.).

Monitor.name: Monitor
Monitor.desc: The Monitor class describes a single instance of a monitor display. They can be accessed from the monitors object.

Network.name: Network
Network.desc: A network is a group of inter-connected Nodes of Operators in one Component. Every component contains a network, and every network lives in a component.

NetworkEditor.name: NetworkEditor
NetworkEditor.desc: The NetworkEditor class describes an instance of a Network Editor. They are subclasses of the Pane Class, which can be accessed from the ui object.

Node.name: Node
Node.desc: A node is the holder of an Operator and all its associated information in a TouchDesigner network. Nodes include information such as flags, inputs and outputs, the OP icon and the OP Viewer.

OP.name: OP
OP.desc: The OP class defines a reference to a single operator.

met.r: if True, allow  Utility nodes  to be returned. If False, Utility nodes will be ignored.       b   =   op  (  'project1'  )
if True, allow  Utility nodes  to be returned. If False, Utility operators will be ignored.
Include custom parameters.       op  (  'player'  )  .  resetPars  (  includeBuiltin  =  False  )   # only reset custom

OP_Viewer_COMP.name: OP Viewer COMP
OP_Viewer_COMP.desc: The OP Viewer Component allows any operator viewer (CHOP Viewer, SOP Viewer, panels, etc.) to be part of a panel with optional full interactivity.

ObjectCOMP.name: ObjectCOMP
ObjectCOMP.desc:
This class inherits from the COMP class.
It is the parent class of these subclasses.

met.s: importABC(filepath, lights=True, cameras=True, mergeGeometry=True, gpuDeform=True, rate=None, textureFolder=None, geometryFolder=None, animationFolder=None)

Page.name: Page
Page.desc: The Page Class describes the list of custom parameters contained on a page. Pages are created on components via the COMP Class. See also the guide Custom Parameters.

met.s: home(zoom: bool = True, op: OP = None) -> None

Panel.name: Panel
Panel.desc: The Panel class manages Panel Components, and is used to access the state of a panel via its Panel Value Class.

PanelCOMP.name: PanelCOMP
PanelCOMP.desc:
The PanelCOMP describes an instance of a Panel Component. The state is represented by Panel Values.
This class inherits from the COMP Class.

met.s: interactMouse(u, v, leftClick=0, middleClick=0, rightClick=0, left=False, middle=False, right=False, wheel=0, pixels=False, Screen=False, quiet=True)

PanelValue.name: PanelValue
PanelValue.desc: A PanelValue describes an instance to a Panel Value.  They can be accessed through a component's panel member, and are used in the Panel Execute DAT.

Par.name: Par
Par.desc: The Par class describes an instance of a single Parameter. See also Custom Parameters.

ParGroup.name: ParGroup
ParGroup.desc: The ParGroup class describes an instance of a single ParGroup.

Parameter.name: Parameter
Parameter.desc: Every operator in TouchDesigner has a set of control Parameters that can be integer or floating point numbers, menus, binary toggles, text strings or operator paths, which determine the output of the operator.

Parent.name: Parent
Parent.desc: In the network path hierarchy, the parent of a node or network is said to be the node or network in which the node is located. So in the network /project1/test/notes, the test node/network is said to be the parent of notes.

Pattern_CHOP.name: Pattern CHOP
Pattern_CHOP.desc: The Pattern CHOP generates a sequence of samples in a channel. Unlike the Wave CHOP its purpose is generating arrays of samples that have no reference to time (seconds or frames).

Peer.name: Peer
Peer.desc: A Peer describes the network connection originating a message in the callback functions found in oscinDAT, tcpipDAT, udpinDAT, udtinDAT.

Point.name: Point
Point.desc: A Point describes an instance to a single geometry point.  They are accessible through the SOP.points member.

Position.name: Position
Position.desc: The position class holds a single 3 component position. A position is a single point in space, and it's important to use a position or vector as appropriate for the data that is being calculated, since matrix operations on them will end in different results. When being multiplied by a Matrix, this c...

Prim.name: Prim
Prim.desc: A Prim describes an instance to a single geometry primitive.  They are accessible through the SOP.prims member.

Project.name: Project
Project.desc: The Project class describes the current session.  It can be accessed with the project object, found in the automatically imported td module. Members changed in this such as the 'paths' member will be written to disk when the project is saved.

PythonOp.name: PythonOp
PythonOp.desc: The PythonOp class represents a Python operator in TouchDesigner, providing access to Python script execution and operator-specific functionality.

Quaternion.name: Quaternion
Quaternion.desc: Holds a Quaternion object which can be used to manipulate rotations in various ways. Quaternions can be constructed using a few different ways to describe the initial rotation:

Run.name: Run
Run.desc:
The Run class describes a single instance of a delayed script execution. See Run Command Examples for more info.
They can be accessed from the runs object. Scripts can be executed with delays with the following methods:

Runs.name: Runs
Runs.desc: The Runs class describes the set of all delayed run objects. It can be accessed with the runs object, found in the automatically imported td module. See Run Command Examples for more info.

SOP.name: SOP
SOP.desc: A SOP describes a reference to a SOP operator, containing points and primitives.

met.r: if True, allow  Utility nodes  to be returned. If False, Utility nodes will be ignored.       b   =   op  (  'project1'  )

ScriptOp.name: ScriptOp
ScriptOp.desc: The ScriptOp class represents script operators in TouchDesigner (Script CHOP, Script DAT, Script SOP, Script TOP), providing specialized functionality for each script operator type.

Segment.name: Segment
Segment.desc: A Segment object describes a single segment from a Timer CHOP.

Sequence.name: Sequence
Sequence.desc: An object describing and controlling a set of sequential parameters. Accessed via

Stype.name: Stype
Stype.desc: Stype is a company that makes RedSpy hardware products that operate with physical cameras and tracks their position, rotation and viewing/lens properties, which, combined with TouchDesigner 3D rendering, enables virtual graphics to be overlaid over live action camera feeds.

SysInfo.name: SysInfo
SysInfo.desc: The SysInfo class describes current system information. Note: It can be accessed with the sysinfo object, found in the automatically imported td module.

TOP.name: TOP
TOP.desc: A TOP describes a reference to a TOP operator.

Timecode.desc: The Timecode class holds a timecode value. See also Timecode and Timecode CHOP.

met.s: setComponents(hour, minute, second, frame, negative=False, fps=None)

met.r: Initializes the Timecode object from a Timecode formatted String: ie.  hh:mm:ss:ff  or  hh:mm:ss.ff
Whether the new Timecode is negative. False by default.
The Timecode's  FPS . If not specified then the  FPS  will not change.

met.s: rates(eg. 29.97). True by default.       n  .  setComponents  (  12  ,   22  ,   33  ,   45  ,   negative  =  True  ,   fps  =  60  )

met.s: messageBox(title: str, message: str, buttons: list = ["Ok"]) -> int

Vertex.name: Vertex
Vertex.desc: A Vertex describes an instance to a single geometry vertex, contained within a Prim object.

td_Module.name: td Module
td_Module.desc: The td module contains all TouchDesigner related Python classes and utilities. All td module members and methods are imported when the application launches and are automatically available in scripts, expressions, and the textport.For additional helpful Python classes and utilities not directly relat...
